title: Reconditioning PCR for removal of PCR bubbles in Illumina librarys
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bp732","text":"This protocol describes how to remove partly single-stranded PCR products (\"PCR bubbles\") from Illumina libraries. For more information about this phenomenon please see this explanation from Illumina. For metabarcoding libraries, it can be hard to estimate the optimal input template or the perfect amount of PCR cycles and therefore overamplification happens frequently. PCR bubbles cannot be quantified reliably with fluorometric-based methods and may look different on different capillary electrophoresis devices. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":169,"length":30}],"data":{}},{"key":"ecpcl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"af7mv","text":"PCR bubbles can lead to failed sequencing runs due to over- or underloading the flowcell.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"88E63379A00E4F019758F5842D1942B0","url":"https://knowledge.illumina.com/library-preparation/general/library-preparation-general-reference_material-list/000001918"}}}}

AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCT - 3'","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ag697","text":"Illumina P7 5' - CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT - 3'","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"82en5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":962409,"guid":"4A32D638030643E1ADFA95FFD9321F82","name":"QIAGEN Multiplex PCR Plus Kit","mol_weight":0,"sku":"206152","url":"https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/discovery-and-translational-research/pcr-qpcr-dpcr/pcr-enzymes-and-kits/end-point-pcr/qiagen-multiplex-pcr-plus-kit","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":25,"name":"Qiagen","link":"https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"notes","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"6pq2q","text":"The mastermix suggested here can be exchanged with another product of choice. If the concentration is different from 2x it has to be adjusted in the Excel sheet.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
steps: Perform a quality control of your library. PCR bubbles may look different depending on the method used for quality control. See below and example of the Fragment Analyzer, Bioanalyzer and an agarose gel. Example result of the Agielnt fragment analyzer for a library that contains PCR bubbles. The desired peak is at 443 bp, the shoulder to the right of it is the PCR bubble. The same library on the Agilent bioanalyzer. The shoulder is moved further to the right, also the relationship of library to PCR bubble changed significantly. PCR reactions of the same library visualized on 1% agarose. Notice the faint band above the actual amplicon.

Concentrate your library by reducing the volume down to 100 µL . We usually do this with a spin-column based protocol, although this can be performed with magnetic beads as well. Please see: PCR cleanup and size selection with magnetic beads by Dominik Buchner or Guanidine-based DNA extraction with silica-coated beads or silica spin columns by Dominik Buchner

Fill the concentration of your library and the project name into the Excel spreadsheet. The suggested master mix for the reconditioning PCR will be calculated accordingly. We usually go for 1250 ng of template input, however, this can be adjusted if necessary. master mix You can download the Excel spreadsheet here: Mastermix Calculator.xlsx If a different mastermix is used, please adjust the concetration in the mastermix calculator.
Perform the PCR with 4 reactions of 50 µL .

Perform a column-based PCR clean-up to exchange the buffer. This can also be done with magnetic beads. Elute the DNA in 100 µL . Guanidine-based DNA extraction with silica-coated beads or silica spin columns by Dominik Buchner
Perform a size selection with a ratio of 0.7x to remove residual primer dimers. Elute the final library in 50 µL . PCR cleanup and size selection with magnetic beads by Dominik Buchner

Perform a final quality control. Quantify the library concentration with a fluorometric-based method and perform quality control via electrophoresis. The shoulder should be gone and the library should be good for sequencing. Example result of the Agielnt fragment analyzer for a library after the removal of PCR bubbles. The same library on the Agilent bioanalyzer after the removal of PCR bubbles. The quality of the sequencing run should be high after the reconditioning PCR.
creator: Dominik Buchner
